# Claw

> Claw is a free, open-source programming language built for AI code
> generation. Every line an AI writes is checked against a live database of
> what actually exists in the project, constrained by a generation grammar,
> typechecked by a real compiler, and verified with executable contracts —
> so hallucinated APIs and silent mistakes cannot reach the developer.

Key facts:
- Made by LambdaQ Labs. License: UPL-1.0. Source: https://github.com/LambdaQ-Labs/claw
- Measured: hallucinated API calls per benchmark run went from 38 to 0 with
  Claw's code database in context; after fine-tuning, 121/121 benchmark
  tasks are hallucination-free including effect declarations.
- Cross-language benchmark (same 0.5B model, functional pass@1, all code
  executed and checked): Claw tuned 94%, JavaScript 89%, Python 56%,
  Rust 35%, Go 7%. Method: https://github.com/LambdaQ-Labs/claw/blob/main/docs/parity-2026-07-05.md
- Works with Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, Zed,
  Gemini CLI, Codex CLI via an MCP server with five tools (symbols,
  candidates, decode grammar, render, real-compiler check).
- Install: curl -fsSL https://clawlang.dev/install.sh | sh

## Docs
- Tour: https://github.com/LambdaQ-Labs/claw/blob/main/docs/tour.md
- MCP setup: https://github.com/LambdaQ-Labs/claw/blob/main/docs/mcp.md
- Benchmark method: https://github.com/LambdaQ-Labs/claw/blob/main/docs/parity-2026-07-05.md
- Telemetry policy (opt-in only): https://github.com/LambdaQ-Labs/claw/blob/main/docs/telemetry.md
